purpose: Pre-register V3C-α1.2 (turnover compression: rebalance cadence 10 → 20 days) before backtests so the result cannot be cherry-picked. α1.2 is a SINGLE-VARIABLE turnover-compression test on top of α1.1: it changes ONLY rebalance_cadence_days (10 → 20) and keeps every other α1.1 knob frozen (4-factor inverse signal, drop rsi_in_band, PIT-liquid top-500, rank-buffer top-20, turnover_budget_per_rebal=1, hold_continuation, same_day_rebuy=false, max_gross=0.50, hard_stop −8%, disabled drawdown breaker, 3 cost scenarios, 6 windows). Per Junyan 2026-05-29 directive, α1.1 cut turnover from α1's 805–959% to 225–322% but still FAILED IMPL4 (≤ 200% = ratio ≤ 2.0). α1.2 isolates the hypothesis: does halving rebalance frequency push turnover ≤ 200% WITHOUT killing α1.1's same-gross α point estimate (full_20yr baseline +0.2187)? If alpha is killed, the edge depended on high-frequency refresh and conditional rebalance (α1.3) is the next lever. If alpha survives AND turnover ≤ 200%, IMPL4 clears and the next layer (α1.3 gross utilization) opens.

variant.single_variable_change: design.portfolio.rebalance_cadence_days: 10 → 20 (all other knobs frozen from α1.1)
variant.hypothesis: Under V3C-α1.1's frozen configuration (10d-horizon 4-factor inverse on PIT-liquid top-500, rank-buffer top-20 + turnover_budget_per_rebal=1 + hold_continuation + same_day_rebuy=false, max_gross=0.50), halving the rebalance cadence from every 10 trading days to every 20 trading days will further reduce annual turnover from α1.1's 225–322% toward the v3 §2 IMPL4 ceiling of 200% (ratio ≤ 2.0), while keeping the same-gross α point estimate within ±50% relative of α1.1's full_20yr baseline (+0.2187). The hypothesis is OPERATIONAL not ECONOMIC — α1.2 does not change the factor causal story, the universe, the cost model, or the sizing; it changes only how often the engine acts on that story. A strategy-gate pass at v3 §2 thresholds remains an independent test that may still FAIL on the unchanged dimensions (IMPL2 gross utilization ~15.6% and IMPL5 MaxDD −54% are explicitly OUT OF α1.2's scope and are deferred to α1.3 and α1.4 respectively). The only validated hard gate this variant targets is turnover_annual_ratio ≤ 2.0 (existing v3 requirement); rebal=20 itself is unvalidated execution-mechanics intuition.
variant.causal_logic_label: Causal logic is questionable/unestablished — this is execution mechanics, not economic alpha proof. The rebal=20 threshold is unvalidated intuition; the ONLY validated hard gate is turnover_annual_ratio ≤ 2.0, which is the existing v3 §2 requirement (Junyan 2026-05-29). The inherited inverse-momentum thesis (from α1 → α1.1) is itself an IC pattern, not an established economic mechanism. α1.2 does not attempt to prove or strengthen the signal; it tests whether a lower action frequency keeps the (weak, not-yet-significant) edge intact while compressing turnover under the gate. Per Junyan: do NOT optimize p-value — let mechanics become compliant and observe whether the signal stabilizes naturally; tuning toward p<0.05 would be curve-fit.
expected_failure_modes: Alpha killed by lower refresh: if α1.1's same-gross α depended on tracking universe drift every 10 days, rebalancing every 20 days lets holdings go stale → same-gross α point drops below +0.11 (50% of α1.1 baseline) → α1.2 rejected, conditional rebalance (α1.3) becomes the next lever instead of fixed rebal=20

Turnover still > 200%: hold_continuation + budget=1 already minimized per-rebal churn under α1.1, so halving rebal frequency may have limited marginal effect — turnover could land at 150–250%, still failing IMPL4. In that case α1.2 is partial-progress, not pass
Walk-forward recent-window degradation: 2018-2022 / 2022-2026 sub-windows (already flat/slightly-negative under α1.1) may worsen with slower adaptation → WF1 point-sign count drops below 3/5
IMPL2 gross utilization (~15.6%) UNCHANGED — out of α1.2 scope, will still FAIL; deferred to α1.3
IMPL5 MaxDD (−54% full_20yr 16yr-underwater episode) UNCHANGED — out of α1.2 scope, will still FAIL; deferred to α1.4

MT BY-corrected family p UNCHANGED-or-WORSE: adding α1.2 to the registered family (now m=3: α1, α1.1, α1.2) makes the BY penalty c(m) larger, so every variant's corrected p RISES. α1.2 will almost certainly still FAIL MT — and that is correct, not a bug; chasing p<0.05 is banned

factor_inputs.name: macd_cross
factor_inputs.normalization: binary 1.0/0.0 from MACD>signal AND signal rising 3 days

factor_inputs.name: rsi_in_band
factor_inputs.normalization: DROPPED per Junyan #8 (α1 inheritance): OOS sign-flip & collapse

factor_inputs.name: limit_up_followup
factor_inputs.normalization: DROPPED per V3A sector-confound + low sector-neutral IC (α1 inheritance)

factor_inputs.name: momentum_5d
factor_inputs.normalization: DROPPED — superseded by momentum_20d 10d horizon (α1 inheritance)

turnover_mechanics.replacement_rule: replace at most 1 existing holding per scheduled rebalance; hard_stop exits do not count against budget, but re-entry waits until next rebalance
turnover_mechanics.hold_continuation_rule: keep existing holdings unless they fall outside top-20 inverse-score candidate buffer

risk_rules.time_stop_status: disabled — superseded by rank_buffer continuation; engine emits sentinel 9999 days for compatibility
risk_rules.limit_up_down_fill_policy: limit-up at T+1 open → BUY unfillable carry; limit-down → SELL unfillable carry
risk_rules.drawdown_breaker_note: v3 research toggle (Junyan PM3 Bug 1): permanent drawdown_to_cash breaker disabled because re-entry-after-N-days logic is not implemented; once triggered the strategy is stuck flat. Production paper trading should leave this False until re-entry logic lands.

hard_gates.primary_target_gate: IMPL4 turnover_annual_ratio ≤ 2.0 — this is the ONE gate α1.2 is designed to move. IMPL2 (gross) and IMPL5 (MaxDD) are explicitly deferred to α1.3/α1.4 and are expected to still FAIL.

mechanics_gates._purpose: Inherited α1.1 mechanics sanity checks (Junyan 2026-05-28 PM5). For α1.2 these are regression guards — they should remain satisfied since the mechanics block is frozen and a LOWER rebal frequency can only reduce churn further. NOT strategy-pass criteria. Strategy gate uses v3 §2 thresholds above.
